title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: SpatialArtifacts R package provides data-driven two-step workflow identify, classify, handle spatial artifacts spatial transcriptomics data multiple platforms including 10x Visium (Standard HD). SpatialArtifacts method combines median absolute deviation (MAD)-based outlier detection morphological image processing. implemented R package within Bioconductor framework, available Bioconductor. artifacts, often appearing areas low gene/UMI counts high mitochondrial ratio tissue edges (edge artifacts) interior (interior artifacts), can negatively impact downstream analyses. guide demonstrates use package real-world datasets across different spatial transcriptomics platforms. details describing method available paper, available bioRxiv.

title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: examples , assume input data provided SpatialExperiment Bioconductor object. case, outputs stored rowData SpatialExperiment object. SpatialArtifacts designed work across multiple spatial transcriptomics platforms: Standard Visium (55µm bins, hexagonal grid): ~5,000 spots per capture area VisiumHD 16µm (16µm bins, square grid): ~480,000 bins per capture area VisiumHD 8µm (8µm bins, square grid): ~1,920,000 bins per capture area morphological detection framework automatically adapts different grid arrangements, parameter scaling critical optimal performance across platforms.

title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: core philosophy two-step process: detect, classify. separates sensitive task identifying potential problem spots nuanced task deciding . first step, use detectEdgeArtifacts() function. , goal identify spots potentially part artifact. work: Outlier identification: Find spots abnormally low QC metrics (e.g., sum_gene) using Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) threshold. 3×3 fill (my_fill): Fill spots completely surrounded outliers 5×5 outline (my_outline): Fill spots outlined outliers larger 16-pixel perimeter Star pattern (my_fill_star): Fill spots outliers four cardinal directions (N, S, E, W) Small cluster removal: Remove isolated normal regions min_cluster_size threshold (default: 40 spots) Use 8-directional connectivity connected component analysis Cluster detection: Group outliers contiguous “problem areas” (problemAreas) Edge identification: Evaluate whether clusters touch tissue boundaries using clumpEdges(). four borders (north, south, east, west), calculates proportion boundary spots belonging cluster. cluster classified edge artifact proportion meets exceeds edge_threshold (default: 0.75, meaning ≥75% border coverage) single border direction output phase add three raw columns spe object: _edge, _problem_id, _problem_size second step, use classifyEdgeArtifacts() function. goal take raw detections previous step apply clear, hierarchical logic assign final labels. work: Input: Requires spe object processed detectEdgeArtifacts() Location: artifact _edge_artifact (TRUE FALSE) based detection step? Size: artifact’s _problem_size larger min_spots (default: 20)? "not_artifact" — High-quality spots "large_edge_artifact" — Large artifact cluster (> min_spots) touching tissue edge "small_edge_artifact" — Small artifact cluster (≤ min_spots) touching tissue edge "large_interior_artifact" — Large artifact cluster (> min_spots) located inside tissue "small_interior_artifact" — Small artifact cluster (≤ min_spots) located inside tissue output phase add one classification column named _classification.

title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: Tuning parameters lets adapt workflow different tissue types, data quality, spatial transcriptomics platforms. package uses wrapper function routes platform-specific implementations. CRITICAL FIRST STEP: Specify platform using platform parameter detectEdgeArtifacts() function: wrapper function accepts platform-specific parameters routed appropriate implementation. platform (REQUIRED) – Character string: "visium" "visiumhd" (case insensitive) Determines platform-specific function use default value; must explicitly specified clarity qc_metric (Default: "sum_gene") – Column name QC metric used outlier detection Common alternatives: "sum_umi", "detected", "nFeature" function auto-detect common variants samples (Default: "sample_id") – Column name sample identifiers sample processed independently mad_threshold (Default: 3) – Sensitivity detecting outliers Lower values (1.5–2) sensitive Higher values (3–4) conservative name (Default: "edge_artifact") – Prefix output column names Outputs: [name]_edge, [name]_problem_id, [name]_problem_size verbose (Default: TRUE) – Print progress messages keep_intermediate (Default: FALSE) – Retain intermediate outlier detection columns platform = "visium", use: edge_threshold (Default: 0.75) – Minimum proportion tissue boundary must occupied outlier clusters (collectively) clusters classified edge artifacts. Important Behavior: threshold applied total coverage outlier clusters boundary direction (North, South, East, West). multiple clusters collectively cover ≥ edge_threshold boundary, clusters touching boundary classified edge artifacts, even single cluster meets threshold individually. Example Scenario: North boundary contains 100 spots total Cluster occupies 30 boundary spots (30% coverage) Cluster B occupies 50 boundary spots (50% coverage) Combined coverage: 80% ≥ 75% threshold Result: Cluster Cluster B classified edge artifacts Rationale: Edge drying artifacts typically affect large, continuous regions along tissue boundaries. Multiple clusters boundary often result single underlying technical failure (incomplete permeabilization) treated unified artifact rather independent events. Tuning Guidance: Higher values (0.75–0.90): conservative, captures large-scale boundary failures Lower values (0.40–0.60): sensitive, may flag smaller boundary-adjacent regions low values (<0.30): Aggressive, may misclassify biological low-expression zones artifacts min_cluster_size (Default: 40) – Minimum cluster size (spots) morphological cleaning focal transformation steps Isolated “normal” regions smaller threshold within outlier areas filled create contiguous artifact regions Standard Visium: 40 spots ≈ 0.12 mm² physical area shifted (Default: FALSE) – Apply coordinate adjustment hexagonal grid alignment Standard Visium typically requires shifted = TRUE using array coordinates Space Ranger. Set FALSE using pre-transformed pixel coordinates VisiumHD square grids. corrects staggered arrangement hexagonal spots batch_var (Default: "") – Determines grouping MAD calculation Options: "sample_id", "slide", "" "": Spots flagged outliers threshold either sample slide grouping platform = "visiumhd", use: Specifies bin size VisiumHD data mandatory VisiumHD; function error provided Determines conversion physical units (µm) bins Defines edge region artifacts expected 16µm resolution: 80 µm → 5 bins 8µm resolution: 80 µm → 10 bins Increase (100-150 µm) tissues larger edge artifacts Decrease (50-60 µm) precise edge detection Clusters smaller filtered morphological cleaning 16µm resolution: 1280 µm² → 5 bins (16×16 µm per bin) 8µm resolution: 1280 µm² → 20 bins (8×8 µm per bin) Physical consistency: area threshold gives different bin counts different resolutions Default (1280 µm²) calibrated 16µm resolution Important: bin indices, pixel coordinates Using bin indices much memory-efficient pixel coordinates Key Difference Visium: VisiumHD parameters specified physical units (µm, µm²) rather bin counts. ensures consistency across resolutions algorithm handles bin conversion internally. classification step platform-independent requires appropriate parameter scaling. min_spots (Default: 20) – CRITICAL PARAMETER: Threshold (number spots/bins) distinguish "large" "small" artifacts Platform-Specific Scaling Required: parameter must adjusted based spatial resolution represent equivalent physical artifact sizes: Automatic Scaling Formula: scaling matters: physical artifact (e.g., 0.1 mm² edge dryspot) cover: Standard Visium: ~33 spots VisiumHD 16µm: ~390 bins VisiumHD 8µm: ~1,560 bins Without scaling, large VisiumHD artifacts incorrectly classified “small.” qc_metric (Default: "sum_umi") – QC metric column validation (must exist directly used classification logic) samples (Default: "sample_id") – Sample ID column name exclude_slides (Default: NULL) – Vector slide IDs exclude edge classification Spots slides edge artifact status set FALSE name (Default: "edge_artifact") – Must match name used detectEdgeArtifacts() functions, several columns added colData(spe): *_edge – Raw detection: spot cluster touching tissue border? (TRUE/FALSE) *_problem_id – Raw detection: ID problem area. *_problem_size – Raw detection: Size (number spots) problem area. *_true_edges — Intermediate: Edge status applying exclude_slides (used classifyEdgeArtifacts()). *_classification — Final classification: One "not_artifact", "large_edge_artifact", "small_edge_artifact", "large_interior_artifact", "small_interior_artifact".
code: # Standard Visium (55µm hexagonal grid) spe <- detectEdgeArtifacts(spe, platform = "visium", shifted = TRUE, ...)  # VisiumHD 16µm (square grid) spe <- detectEdgeArtifacts(spe, platform = "visiumhd", resolution = "16um", ...)  # VisiumHD 8µm (square grid) spe <- detectEdgeArtifacts(spe, platform = "visiumhd", resolution = "8um", ...) min_spots_HD <- min_spots_visium × (55 / bin_size_µm)²  # Example: For min_spots = 30 on Standard Visium # VisiumHD 16µm: 30 × (55/16)² ≈ 354 bins # VisiumHD 8µm:  30 × (55/8)²  ≈ 1,420 bins

title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: package includes spe_vignette, lightweight SpatialExperiment object derived human hippocampus Visium sample. vignette load raw-like object run full SpatialArtifacts workflow live. Note: meet package size requirements (< 5MB), object subset (e.g., coding genes) sparsified, artifact detection run. perform steps now. underlying spatial clustering functions package currently require dense matrix perform coordinate-based calculations. must first convert sparse counts assay spe_vignette object standard (dense) matrix. VisiumHD data, workflow identical except parameter scaling. ’s complete example showing adapt parameters VisiumHD: Platform-Specific Function Calls: Parameter Recommendations Platform: ’ll create comprehensive visualization showing QC metrics, detection results, detailed cluster information:  Let’s examine enhanced classification system: Finally, let’s validate flagged spots lower quality metrics:  Based new classifications, users can make informed decisions filtering. example, might decide remove edge artifacts (large small) keeping interior artifacts review. ’s can filter SpatialExperiment object remove spots classified "large_edge_artifact" "small_edge_artifact":  vignette demonstrated SpatialArtifacts workflow across multiple spatial transcriptomics platforms. Specifically, showed: Standard Visium workflow: Complete example using included hippocampus dataset VisiumHD adaptation: properly scale parameters 16µm 8µm resolution data Platform-specific considerations: Grid arrangement, parameter scaling formulas, typical artifact sizes Visualization: Display hierarchical classification results alongside QC metrics Classification logic: Distinguish edge vs. interior large vs. small artifacts Validation: Flagged spots show significantly lower molecular capture Key Takeaways Multi-Platform Usage: Grid Structure: Standard Visium uses hexagonal grids (shifted = TRUE), VisiumHD uses square grids (shifted = FALSE) Critical Parameter Scaling: min_spots threshold classifyEdgeArtifacts() must scale spatial resolution: Use formula: min_spots_HD = min_spots_visium × (55 / bin_size)² Without scaling, artifacts incorrectly classified size Morphological Framework: detection logic works across platforms, automatically adapting different grid arrangements Physical Consistency: Scaled parameters ensure “large” “small” artifact classifications represent equivalent physical sizes regardless platform Overall, SpatialArtifacts provides unified, platform-agnostic framework detecting classifying spatial artifacts, enabling consistent quality control across evolving spatial transcriptomics technology landscape.

VisiumHD uses square grid (no 'shifted' needed) spe_hd16_detected <- detectEdgeArtifacts(   spe_hd16,   platform = "visiumhd", # Specify VisiumHD platform   resolution = "16um", # REQUIRED for VisiumHD   qc_metric = "sum_umi", # or "sum" depending on your colData   samples = "sample_id",   buffer_width_um = 100, # VisiumHD-specific parameter   mad_threshold = 2.5,   edge_threshold = 0.75,   name = "edge_artifact" )  # Step 3: Classification Phase - Detected Genes") +     base_theme +     coord_fixed() } else {   p2 <- ggplot() +     theme_void() +     ggtitle("B. Detected Genes (Data N/A)") }  # Plot 3: Raw Detection (Any Problem Area) if ("edge_artifact_problem_id" %in% names(plot_data_in_tissue)) {   p3 <- ggplot(plot_data_in_tissue, aes(     x = pxl_col_in_fullres, y = pxl_row_in_fullres,     color = !is.na(edge_artifact_problem_id)   )) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_manual(       values = c("FALSE" = "lightgray", "TRUE" = "red"),       name = "Problem?"     ) +     ggtitle("C. Raw Detection (All Problem Areas)") +     base_theme +     coord_fixed() } else {   p3 <- ggplot() +     theme_void() +     ggtitle("C. Raw Detection (Data N/A)") }  # Plot 4: Cluster IDs if ("edge_artifact_problem_id" %in% names(plot_data_in_tissue)) {   plot_data_in_tissue$cluster_display <- NA    has_cluster <- !is.na(plot_data_in_tissue$edge_artifact_problem_id)   plot_data_in_tissue$cluster_display[has_cluster] <-     plot_data_in_tissue$edge_artifact_problem_id[has_cluster]    n_clusters <- length(unique(plot_data_in_tissue$cluster_display[has_cluster]))    p4 <- ggplot(plot_data_in_tissue, aes(     x = pxl_col_in_fullres, y = pxl_row_in_fullres,     color = cluster_display   )) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_discrete(name = "Cluster ID", na.value = "lightgray") +     ggtitle(paste0("D. Problem Area Clusters (n=", n_clusters, ")")) +     base_theme +     coord_fixed() +     theme(       legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm"),       legend.text = element_text(size = 8)     ) } else {   p4 <- ggplot() +     theme_void() +     ggtitle("D. text: VisiumHD data, workflow identical except parameter scaling. ’s complete example showing adapt parameters VisiumHD: Platform-Specific Function Calls: Parameter Recommendations Platform:
code: # This is a pseudo-example demonstrating VisiumHD 16µm workflow # Assumes you have loaded a VisiumHD SpatialExperiment object as 'spe_hd16'  # Step 1: Ensure required QC metrics are calculated library(scuttle) spe_hd16 <- addPerCellQCMetrics(spe_hd16)  # Step 2: Detection Phase - VisiumHD uses square grid (no 'shifted' needed) spe_hd16_detected <- detectEdgeArtifacts(   spe_hd16,   platform = "visiumhd", # Specify VisiumHD platform   resolution = "16um", # REQUIRED for VisiumHD   qc_metric = "sum_umi", # or "sum" depending on your colData   samples = "sample_id",   buffer_width_um = 100, # VisiumHD-specific parameter   mad_threshold = 2.5,   edge_threshold = 0.75,   name = "edge_artifact" )  # Step 3: Classification Phase - CRITICAL: Scale min_spots for VisiumHD resolution # For 16µm bins, use ~6-10× the Standard Visium threshold min_spots_16um <- 30 * (55 / 16)^2 # ≈ 354 bins  spe_hd16_classified <- classifyEdgeArtifacts(   spe_hd16_detected,   qc_metric = "sum_umi",   min_spots = round(min_spots_16um), # ~350 bins   name = "edge_artifact" )  # Visualization (same approach as Standard Visium) table(spe_hd16_classified$edge_artifact_classification) # This is a pseudo-example demonstrating VisiumHD 8µm workflow # Assumes you have loaded a VisiumHD 8µm SpatialExperiment object as 'spe_hd8'  # Step 1: QC metrics spe_hd8 <- addPerCellQCMetrics(spe_hd8)  # Step 2: Detection Phase spe_hd8_detected <- detectEdgeArtifacts(   spe_hd8,   platform = "visiumhd", # Specify VisiumHD platform   resolution = "8um", # REQUIRED: Specify 8µm resolution   qc_metric = "sum_umi",   samples = "sample_id",   buffer_width_um = 100, # Buffer zone in micrometers   mad_threshold = 2.5,   edge_threshold = 0.75,   name = "edge_artifact" )  # Step 3: Classification with 8µm-appropriate threshold # For 8µm bins, use ~20-40× the Standard Visium threshold min_spots_8um <- 30 * (55 / 8)^2 # ≈ 1,420 bins  spe_hd8_classified <- classifyEdgeArtifacts(   spe_hd8_detected,   qc_metric = "sum_umi",   min_spots = round(min_spots_8um), # ~1,400 bins   name = "edge_artifact" )  table(spe_hd8_classified$edge_artifact_classification)

in_tissue_data <- spe_classified[, spe_classified$in_tissue]  qc_data <- data.frame(   sum_umi = in_tissue_data$sum_umi,   detected_genes = in_tissue_data$detected,   flagged = in_tissue_data$edge_artifact_edge )  # --- Calculate Median Differences --- flagged_umi <- median(qc_data$sum_umi[qc_data$flagged], na.rm = TRUE) nonflagged_umi <- median(qc_data$sum_umi[!qc_data$flagged], na.rm = TRUE)  flagged_gene <- median(qc_data$detected_genes[qc_data$flagged], na.rm = TRUE) nonflagged_gene <- median(qc_data$detected_genes[!qc_data$flagged], na.rm = TRUE)  cat("QC Validation Results (comparing raw edge detection flag):
") #> QC Validation Results (comparing raw edge detection flag): cat("Flagged spots - Median UMI:", round(flagged_umi), "
") #> Flagged spots - Median UMI: 120 cat("Non-flagged spots - Median UMI:", round(nonflagged_umi), "
") #> Non-flagged spots - Median UMI: 1784 cat("UMI difference:", round(nonflagged_umi - flagged_umi), "

") #> UMI difference: 1664  cat("Flagged spots - Median Detected Genes:", round(flagged_gene), "
") #> Flagged spots - Median Detected Genes: 106 cat("Non-flagged spots - Median Detected Genes:", round(nonflagged_gene), "
") #> Non-flagged spots - Median Detected Genes: 1019 cat("Gene difference:", round(nonflagged_gene - flagged_gene), "
") #> Gene difference: 912  qc_data$flag_status <- ifelse(qc_data$flagged, "Flagged (Raw Edge)", "Non-Flagged")  validation_plot <- ggplot(qc_data, aes(x = flag_status, y = log10(sum_umi + 1), fill = flag_status)) +   geom_boxplot() +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Flagged (Raw Edge)" = "lightcoral", "Non-Flagged" = "lightblue")) +   labs(     title = "QC Validation: UMI Counts in Raw Edge vs Non-Edge Spots",     x = "Raw Edge Detection Status", y = "log10(UMI Count + 1)"   ) +   theme_minimal()  print(validation_plot)

title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: Based new classifications, users can make informed decisions filtering. example, might decide remove edge artifacts (large small) keeping interior artifacts review. ’s can filter SpatialExperiment object remove spots classified "large_edge_artifact" "small_edge_artifact":
code:
if ("edge_artifact_classification" %in% names(colData(spe_classified))) {   spots_to_keep <- !spe_classified$edge_artifact_classification %in%     c("large_edge_artifact", "small_edge_artifact")   spe_filtered <- spe_classified[, spots_to_keep]   cat("Original number of spots:", ncol(spe_classified), "
")   cat("Number of spots after filtering:", ncol(spe_filtered), "
") } else {   cat("Classification column not found. Filtering step skipped.
") } #> Original number of spots: 4965  #> Number of spots after filtering: 4891 plot_data_before <- as.data.frame(colData(spe_classified)) coords_mat <- spatialCoords(spe_classified) plot_data_before <- cbind(plot_data_before, as.data.frame(coords_mat)) plot_data_before_in_tissue <- plot_data_before[plot_data_before$in_tissue, ]  plot_data_after <- as.data.frame(colData(spe_filtered)) if (ncol(spe_filtered) > 0) {   coords_mat_after <- spatialCoords(spe_filtered)   plot_data_after <- cbind(plot_data_after, as.data.frame(coords_mat_after)) }  base_theme <- theme_void() +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5),     legend.position = "right"   )  p1_umi_before <- ggplot(plot_data_before_in_tissue, aes(x = pxl_col_in_fullres, y = pxl_row_in_fullres, color = log10(sum_umi + 1))) +   geom_point(size = 0.5) +   scale_color_viridis_c(name = "log10(UMI)") +   ggtitle("UMI Counts (Before Filtering)") +   base_theme +   coord_fixed()  if (ncol(spe_filtered) > 0) {   p2_umi_after <- ggplot(plot_data_after, aes(x = pxl_col_in_fullres, y = pxl_row_in_fullres, color = log10(sum_umi + 1))) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_viridis_c(name = "log10(UMI)") +     ggtitle("UMI Counts (After Filtering)") +     base_theme +     coord_fixed() } else {   p2_umi_after <- ggplot() +     theme_void() +     ggtitle("UMI Counts (After Filtering - No Spots)") }  p3_class_before <- ggplot(plot_data_before_in_tissue, aes(x = pxl_col_in_fullres, y = pxl_row_in_fullres, color = edge_artifact_classification)) +   geom_point(size = 0.5) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c(       "not_artifact" = "lightgray",       "large_edge_artifact" = "red",       "small_edge_artifact" = "orange",       "large_interior_artifact" = "blue",       "small_interior_artifact" = "cyan"     ),     name = "Final Class",     na.value = "grey50",     drop = FALSE   ) +   ggtitle("Classification (Before Filtering)") +   base_theme +   coord_fixed()  if (ncol(spe_filtered) > 0) {   p4_class_after <- ggplot(plot_data_after, aes(x = pxl_col_in_fullres, y = pxl_row_in_fullres, color = edge_artifact_classification)) +     geom_point(size = 0.5) +     scale_color_manual(       values = c(         "not_artifact" = "lightgray",         "large_edge_artifact" = "red",         "small_edge_artifact" = "orange",         "large_interior_artifact" = "blue",         "small_interior_artifact" = "cyan"       ),       name = "Final Class",       na.value = "grey50",       drop = FALSE     ) +     ggtitle("Classification (After Filtering)") +     base_theme +     coord_fixed() } else {   p4_class_after <- ggplot() +     theme_void() +     ggtitle("Classification (After Filtering - No Spots)") }  combined_filtering_plot_2x2 <- (p1_umi_before | p2_umi_after) / (p3_class_before | p4_class_after) print(combined_filtering_plot_2x2)

title: SpatialArtifacts Tutorial
text: vignette demonstrated SpatialArtifacts workflow across multiple spatial transcriptomics platforms. Specifically, showed: Standard Visium workflow: Complete example using included hippocampus dataset VisiumHD adaptation: properly scale parameters 16µm 8µm resolution data Platform-specific considerations: Grid arrangement, parameter scaling formulas, typical artifact sizes Visualization: Display hierarchical classification results alongside QC metrics Classification logic: Distinguish edge vs. interior large vs. small artifacts Validation: Flagged spots show significantly lower molecular capture Key Takeaways Multi-Platform Usage: Grid Structure: Standard Visium uses hexagonal grids (shifted = TRUE), VisiumHD uses square grids (shifted = FALSE) Critical Parameter Scaling: min_spots threshold classifyEdgeArtifacts() must scale spatial resolution: Use formula: min_spots_HD = min_spots_visium × (55 / bin_size)² Without scaling, artifacts incorrectly classified size Morphological Framework: detection logic works across platforms, automatically adapting different grid arrangements Physical Consistency: Scaled parameters ensure “large” “small” artifact classifications represent equivalent physical sizes regardless platform Overall, SpatialArtifacts provides unified, platform-agnostic framework detecting classifying spatial artifacts, enabling consistent quality control across evolving spatial transcriptomics technology landscape.

Note: column must exist, directly used classification logic. samples Character string specifying sample ID column name (default: "sample_id"). min_spots Minimum number spots artifact classified "large" (default: 20). name Character string matching name argument used detectEdgeArtifacts() (default: "edge_artifact"). exclude_slides Character vector slide IDs exclude edge detection (default: NULL). title: Classify Edge Artifacts into Hierarchical Categories — classifyEdgeArtifacts
text: SpatialExperiment object additional classification columns colData: [name]_true_edges: Logical, indicating edge artifacts applying slide exclusions. [name]_classification: Character, containing hierarchical categories: "not_artifact": Normal, high-quality spots. "large_edge_artifact": Clusters > min_spots touching slide boundary. "small_edge_artifact": Clusters <= min_spots touching slide boundary. "large_interior_artifact": Clusters > min_spots tissue interior. "small_interior_artifact": Clusters <= min_spots tissue interior.

title: Classify Edge Artifacts into Hierarchical Categories — classifyEdgeArtifacts
text: Parameter Recommendations: min_spots threshold scale platform resolution represent similar physical artifact sizes: Standard Visium (55µm bins): min_spots = 20-40 Physical area: ~0.06-0.12 mm² Rationale: Artifacts <20 spots likely represent isolated noise Typical edge artifacts: 50-200 spots VisiumHD 16µm bins: min_spots = 100-200 Physical area: ~0.026-0.051 mm² (comparable Visium) Rationale: Higher density requires proportionally higher threshold Typical edge artifacts: 500-2000 bins VisiumHD 8µm bins: min_spots = 400-800 Physical area: ~0.026-0.051 mm² (comparable Visium) Rationale: 4× density 16µm bins requires 4× threshold Typical edge artifacts: 2000-8000 bins Practical Guideline: VisiumHD data, start : min_spots = 20 × (55µm / bin_size)² formula maintains constant physical area thresholds across resolutions. Context-Specific Tuning: Increase min_spots noisy data (prevents -flagging small clusters) Decrease min_spots high-quality data (captures smaller genuine artifacts) Visualize intermediate results validate threshold appropriateness

#>   Total edge spots: 4 #>   Total problem area spots: 4  # Check detection results table(spe_detected$edge_artifact_edge) #>  #> FALSE  TRUE  #>    12     4  head(colData(spe_detected)[, c(   "edge_artifact_edge",   "edge_artifact_problem_id" )]) #> DataFrame with 6 rows and 2 columns #>        edge_artifact_edge edge_artifact_problem_id #>                 <logical>              <character> #> spot_1               TRUE            mock_sample_1 #> spot_2               TRUE            mock_sample_1 #> spot_3               TRUE            mock_sample_1 #> spot_4               TRUE            mock_sample_1 #> spot_5              FALSE                       NA #> spot_6              FALSE                       NA

code: library(SpatialExperiment) library(S4Vectors)  # 1. Runnable Example: Mock Data (Try this!)  # Create a mock Visium HD dataset (20x20 grid, representing 320x320 um) n_rows <- 20 n_cols <- 20 n_bins <- n_rows * n_cols coords <- expand.grid(array_row = 1:n_rows, array_col = 1:n_cols)  # Simulate gene counts: Edge artifact (left 2 cols) has low counts counts <- rep(100, n_bins) is_edge <- coords$array_col <= 2 counts[is_edge] <- 10  # Create SpatialExperiment object spe_hd <- SpatialExperiment(   assays = list(counts = matrix(counts, nrow = 1, ncol = n_bins)),   colData = DataFrame(     sample_id = "mock_hd",     in_tissue = rep(TRUE, n_bins),     sum_gene = counts,     array_row = coords$array_row,     array_col = coords$array_col   ) )  # Run detection for 16um resolution # (Physical buffer 80um / 16um bin size = 5 bins. Artifact is 2 bins wide.)  colnames(spe_hd) <- paste0("spot_", seq_len(ncol(spe_hd))) rownames(spe_hd) <- paste0("gene_", seq_len(nrow(spe_hd)))  spe_hd <- detectEdgeArtifacts_VisiumHD(   spe_hd,   resolution = "16um",   qc_metric = "sum_gene" ) #> ================================================================ #> VisiumHD Edge Artifact Detection #> ================================================================ #> Resolution: 16um (bin size = 16*16 um) #> Coordinate Range: X[1-20], Y[1-20] (bin indices) #> Buffer Width: 80 um -> 5 bins #> Min Cluster Area: 1280 um2 -> 5 bins #> Samples: 1 #>  #> --- STEP 1: Outlier Detection --- #>   Total outliers detected: 40 bins #>  #> --- STEP 2: Edge Artifact Detection (Buffer Zone) --- #>   mock_hd: 40 edge artifact bins #>  #> --- STEP 3: Interior Problem Area Detection --- #>   Method: Morphological processing + clustering #>     Clustering 40 outlier bins... #>     Creating raster: 20 rows * 20 cols #>     Applying morphological transformations... #>     [16um Mode] Adjusted min_cluster_size: 5 -> 2 #>     Clustering outlier regions... #>     Found 40 clustered cells #>     Identified 1 unique clusters #>     Kept 1 clusters (>=2 bins): 40 bins total #>   mock_hd: 0 interior bins in 0 clusters (1 edge filtered) #>  #> ================================================================ #> Detection Complete! #> ================================================================ #> Total outliers detected:      40 bins #> Edge artifacts (in buffer):   40 bins #> Interior problem areas:       0 bins #> Classification rate:          100.0% #> ================================================================  # Check results table(spe_hd$edge_artifact_edge) #>  #> FALSE  TRUE  #>   360    40   # 2. Illustrative Examples (Concept only) if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming 'spe' is a real SpatialExperiment object  # 8um data with defaults # buffer_width_um = 80 -> 10 bins (80 / 8) # min_cluster_area_um2 = 1280 -> 20 bins spe <- detectEdgeArtifacts_VisiumHD(spe, resolution = "8um")  # 16um data with defaults # buffer_width_um = 80 -> 5 bins (80 / 16) # min_cluster_area_um2 = 1280 -> 5 bins spe <- detectEdgeArtifacts_VisiumHD(spe, resolution = "16um")  # Custom parameters (physical units) spe <- detectEdgeArtifacts_VisiumHD(   spe,   resolution = "16um",   buffer_width_um = 100, # 100 $u$m buffer   min_cluster_area_um2 = 2000 # 2000 $u$m^2 minimum ) } # }
